metadata.name: Prototype — Blitz Automaton cast with prototype cost {2}{R}, enters as 3/2 red with Haste, attacks for 3
metadata.description: P1 casts Blitz Automaton using its Prototype cost {2}{R} (CR 702.160, CR 718.3). Per CR 718.3b the spell has the prototype's power and toughness (3/2) and the prototype's colors (red) in addition to its normal types and abilities. The permanent enters the battlefield as a 3/2 red Artifact Creature with Haste. P1 then attacks with it — P2 has no blockers — and the unblocked 3/2 deals 3 combat damage to P2 (life 40 to 37). Key rule: Prototype is NOT an alternative cost (CR 718.3); the engine signals it via Command::CastSpell { prototype: true }. The script documents the harness gap (no 'cast_spell_prototype' action type) in the disputes array.
metadata.generation_notes: Blitz Automaton card definition (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/blitz_automaton.rs): mana_cost {7}, types Artifact+Creature (Construct), power 6 / toughness 4. Abilities: AbilityDefinition::Keyword(Prototype) + AbilityDefinition::Prototype { cost: ManaCost { generic: 2, red: 1 }, power: 3, toughness: 2 } + AbilityDefinition::Keyword(Haste). CR 702.160: 'Prototype' is a static ability that lets you cast the spell with different mana cost and characteristics. CR 718.3: When cast using the prototype cost, the spell's base power and toughness are the prototype values, and the spell's color is determined by the prototype cost. CR 718.3b: The permanent that enters the battlefield has the prototype power, toughness, and color. Per CR 718.3 (important): Prototype is NOT an alternative cost (CR 601.2b); it is a separate casting option that modifies the spell's characteristics. The engine uses Command::CastSpell { prototype: true } to signal prototype casting. The harness action type needed is 'cast_spell_prototype' — analogous to 'cast_spell_blitz', 'cast_spell_evoke', etc. HARNESS GAP: As of this script's generation, replay_harness.rs has no 'cast_spell_prototype' match arm in translate_player_action(); the prototype field is always false. The script models the correct CR behavior; the harness gap is documented as a dispute. mana_paid: { colorless: 2, red: 1 } (prototype cost {2}{R} = 2 generic + 1 red). After resolution: 3/2 red Artifact Creature with Haste on P1's battlefield. P1 attacks; P2 has no creatures; unblocked 3/2 deals 3 damage to P2 (life 40 to 37). Haste (CR 702.10b) means the creature can attack the turn it entered. Combat steps follow the blitz/dash/Riveteers pattern established in script_stack_133.

disputes.description: Harness gap: 'cast_spell_prototype' is not a recognized action type in replay_harness.rs translate_player_action(). The match arm for prototype casting does not exist; all paths set prototype: false. The engine Command::CastSpell has a prototype field (bool) that the casting subsystem uses to apply CR 718.3 characteristic modifications. The fix is to add a 'cast_spell_prototype' arm to translate_player_action() that finds the card in the player's hand and builds Command::CastSpell { prototype: true, alt_cost: None, ... } with mana_paid reflecting the prototype cost. The script action and its CR citations are correct per CR 702.160 and CR 718.3; only the harness execution path is missing.

script.step_note: P1 casts Blitz Automaton using its Prototype cost {2}{R} (CR 702.160, CR 718.3). Per CR 718.3b the spell is 3/2 and red while on the stack. Both players pass priority. The spell resolves: Blitz Automaton enters the battlefield as a 3/2 red Artifact Creature with Haste (prototype characteristics). Both players pass priority with empty stack to advance to beginning_of_combat.

actions.description: Initial: Blitz Automaton in P1 hand; no permanents on battlefield; P1 has {2}{R} in mana pool; P2 at 40 life; stack empty
actions.note: Blitz Automaton ({7}, 6/4 Artifact Creature — Construct, Haste, Prototype {2}{R} — 3/2) is in P1's hand. P1 has {2}{R} (colorless:2, red:1) to pay the prototype cost. No permanents in play.

CR 702.160: P1 casts Blitz Automaton using its Prototype ability. CR 718.3: The prototype cost {2}{R} is paid (colorless:2 + red:1). The spell is NOT treated as an alternative cost (CR 718.3 explicitly: prototype is not an alternative cost per CR 601.2b). CR 718.3b: While on the stack and when it resolves, the permanent has power 3, toughness 2, and color red (from the prototype cost). The card's other characteristics (type: Artifact Creature — Construct, Haste keyword) are unchanged. CR 601.2: Spell moves from P1's hand to the stack.

actions.description: After prototype cast: Blitz Automaton on stack (count 1); P1 hand empty
actions.note: CR 601.2: Casting moves Blitz Automaton from P1's hand to the stack. The prototype cost {2}{R} has been paid. P1's hand is now empty.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Blitz Automaton (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 718.3b: Blitz Automaton enters the battlefield with its prototype characteristics: power 3, toughness 2, color red. It retains its card types (Artifact Creature — Construct), its name, and all abilities including Haste. CR 702.10b: Haste means the permanent is not affected by summoning sickness and may attack this turn.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Creature spell resolves — permanent enters battlefield under P1's control. CR 718.3b: The permanent has prototype P/T (3/2) and prototype color (red) for as long as the Prototype effect applies. CR 702.10b: Haste is active.

actions.description: After resolution: Blitz Automaton (3/2, red, Haste) on P1 battlefield; stack empty; life totals unchanged
actions.note: CR 718.3b: Blitz Automaton is on the battlefield as a 3/2 red Artifact Creature — Construct with Haste. The prototype cost was paid so the permanent uses the prototype's power (3), toughness (2), and color (red) rather than the original {7} card's 6/4 colorless. Haste (CR 702.10b) allows it to attack the turn it entered.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance from precombat_main to beginning_of_combat step.

script.step_note: Beginning of combat step. No triggered abilities fire. Both players pass priority to advance to declare_attackers.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in beginning_of_combat with empty stack — advance to declare_attackers step.

script.step_note: P1 declares Blitz Automaton as an attacker targeting P2. The 3/2 has Haste so it may attack the turn it entered (CR 702.10b). It taps (CR 508.1f). Both players pass priority to advance to declare_blockers.

actions.note: CR 508.1: P1 declares Blitz Automaton as an attacking creature targeting P2. CR 702.10b: Haste granted by the card's Haste keyword — summoning sickness does not apply (Blitz Automaton has Haste regardless of whether it was cast with the prototype cost). CR 508.1f: Creature taps as a side effect of attacking. CR 508.1k: Becomes an attacking creature.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Blitz Automaton tapped (attacking P2); stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Blitz Automaton tapped as part of the attack declaration. It is now an attacking creature targeting P2.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: P2 declares no blockers (P2 controls no creatures). Blitz Automaton is unblocked (CR 509.1h). Both players pass priority — advance to combat_damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P2 declares no blockers — P2 controls no creatures. CR 509.1h: Blitz Automaton has no blockers declared; it becomes an unblocked attacker.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to combat_damage step.

script.step_note: Blitz Automaton (3/2, unblocked) assigns 3 combat damage to P2 (CR 510.1b). P2 life: 40 to 37. No triggered abilities from damage. Both players pass priority to advance to end_of_combat.

actions.note: CR 510.1: P1 assigns all 3 points of combat damage from Blitz Automaton (prototype power 3) to P2 — it is unblocked (CR 510.1b). CR 718.3b: The prototype power 3 is used for combat damage (not the original 6/4). CR 120.3: P2 loses 3 life.

actions.description: After combat damage: P2 at 37 life; Blitz Automaton on P1 battlefield (tapped); stack empty
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Blitz Automaton (prototype power 3, unblocked) dealt 3 combat damage to P2. P2 life: 40 to 37. CR 718.3b: The 3/2 prototype characteristics — not the 6/4 base card — determine combat damage. No triggered abilities from damage. Stack is empty.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to end_of_combat step.

script.step_note: End of combat step. No triggered abilities fire (Blitz Automaton has no end-of-combat triggers). Both players pass priority to conclude combat.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in end_of_combat with empty stack — combat phase ends. Unlike Blitz or Dash, Prototype does not create a sacrifice trigger at the end step (CR 718.3). The permanent remains on the battlefield after combat.

actions.description: Final: Blitz Automaton still on P1 battlefield (tapped); P2 at 37 life; P1 at 40 life; stack empty
actions.note: CR 718.3b: Blitz Automaton (cast with prototype {2}{R}) remains on the battlefield as a 3/2 red Artifact Creature — Construct with Haste. It is tapped (attacked this turn). Prototype does NOT cause it to return to hand or be sacrificed; unlike Dash (CR 702.109a) and Blitz (CR 702.152a), there is no end-of-step trigger (CR 718.3). P2 is at 37 life (lost 3 from combat damage). The scenario validates: (1) prototype casting uses the prototype cost, (2) the permanent enters with prototype P/T and color, and (3) prototype power determines combat damage.
